summary: Drop `sync` and `Analytics Over Mirror` from .printing-press.json novel_features and document in defaultSyncResources why the resource list is empty for Etherpad.
reason: Etherpad's HTTP API is RPC-shaped, not REST-shaped: every list endpoint (`listAllPads`, `listAllGroups`, `listAuthorsOfPad`, `listSessionsOfAuthor`, ...) returns a wrapper of scalar IDs (`{"data":{"padIDs":["id1","id2",...]}}`), not arrays of objects with extractable primary-key fields. Hydrating each ID into a record requires a separate per-resource RPC call per item (e.g., `getRevisionsCount`, `getLastEdited`). The generator's sync template assumes paginated object lists, so `defaultSyncResources()` and `syncResourcePath()` ship as empty stubs — the command exits 0 with 0 records by default, and any `--resources <name>` invocation returns `unknown sync resource`. Advertising sync/analytics as novel features in this state was misleading (Greptile P1 on PR #396). The `sync` and `analytics` commands themselves remain in the binary so a future patch can wire in a fan-out adapter, but they are not part of the published feature set.
validated_outcome: novel_features now lists only the four commands that work end-to-end against Etherpad's API shape: tail, workflow archive, import, agent-context. defaultSyncResources() carries an inline comment explaining the Etherpad-specific gap so the next regen reviewer doesn't try to remove the comment as dead code.
upstream_issue: TODO: file on mvanhorn/cli-printing-press — generator template should detect APIs that return only scalar-ID lists (no object hydration) and either elide sync/analytics from novel_features automatically or emit a per-resource fan-out adapter.
